actions: Address Lodi urgently — schedule a call this week, clarify unresolved issues, consider increasing check-in frequency
Get Sierra Vista to a go-live plan — identify the blocker (3,529 drafts, 0 published) and set a firm target date
Investigate low shift invite fill rate (2-4%) — check app adoption at top facilities and consider re-engagement push
Drive NPS submissions — only 3 responses across 35+ facilities; expand outreach to get 5-6 live facility responses
Build TX-batch onboarding roadmap — ~25 facilities not started; sequence with Marcus over Q2
entries.notes: Large portfolio in active expansion. Marcus relationship is healthy and forward-looking (API/analytics discussions). NPS dragging score down significantly — avg 1.67/5 from 3 responses. Lodi and Sierra Vista both flagged at-risk in Slack. Fill rate at 2-4% is a concern. ~25 TX-suffix facilities not yet started.

platformUsage.summary: 10 of 14 facilities publishing shifts. Messaging volume 160x growth Oct-Mar. Fill rate 2-4% — weak engagement with shift invites. 4 facilities at zero published shifts.
highlights: Lodi, Tracy, University Post-Acute, Oakland Heights: 0 published shifts

npsSurvey.trend: Only 3 responses total — insufficient for trend

supportLoad.summary: 2 HIGH open (Shared Calendar View + facility call off FR). Multiple MEDIUM bugs including overtime calculations and web privacy issue. Active backlog with no recent resolutions.

onboardingHealth.summary: 5 live, 4 in soft-launch/training, 4 in early setup, ~27 not started (TX-suffix batch). Large pipeline ahead.

customerSentiment.summary: Marcus engaged and expanding — API/analytics integration discussions underway. No escalations in transcripts. Lodi is the one substantive risk signal. Facilities going live reporting positive experiences.
signals: Marcus pursuing UKG API + billing + analytics integration — strong commitment signal
Multiple facilities successfully onboarded in Q1 2026 with positive staff adoption
Lodi flagged at-risk — contact frustrated with rep time allocation
Sierra Vista flagged at-risk — no clear go-live path

engagementCadence.summary: Strong weekly/biweekly cadence across all active facilities. Corporate sync with Marcus Feb 24 + API meeting Mar 20. No facilities have gone dark.

bugTicketUrls.label: Agency appears on schedule twice when they have a called off shift
Schedule doesn't display properly when area is secondary grouping

featureRequestUrls.label: Staff schedule preferences for overnight shifts
Still require scheduler approval after staff accepts shift invite
Change the display/print order of shift templates
Share open shifts that aren't published
Submit pending time off requests from scheduler side

actions: NPS remediation: Kei-Ai LA scored 2/5 and Country Crest scored 1/5 in Jan–Feb — get on a call with both building contacts before the May 14 QBR to understand root causes and close the loop.

The Avenues is near-dark: 2.6% published despite being fully live since Jan. Escalate to building admin immediately — Feb 10 notes flag schedule visibility issues. This is the highest-urgency operational risk.
entries.notes: Large enterprise account — 42 facilities in pipeline, 8 live. Strong exec engagement (President + CEO + CAO in contact, all touched within 90 days). Q1 QBR scheduled May 14. Key risks: low NPS on first two live buildings, The Avenues near-dark, and rapidly escalating overtime spend ($1.5M/mo) that Veras hasn't yet demonstrably bent. UKG sync gap noted Feb 10.

productAdoption.summary: 19 facilities active in Metabase; 8 live, 11 in onboarding. Overall shift-weighted publish rate 45.6%. Riverwood (93%) and Country Crest (85%) lead. The Avenues (2.6%) is critically underperforming for a live building. Nursing engagement strong: 83% 30-day login, 89% mobile adoption. Scheduler headcount grew slightly (+2.2% MoM). Fill rate via ShiftInvite very low at 1.1% — staff largely not accepting open shift invitations through app yet.
highlights: The Avenues: 2.6% published — LIVE building, urgent
ShiftInvite fill rate 1.1% (Apr) — staff not using open shift accept flow

outcomesRoi.summary: 927 hours saved in trailing 12 months (1,688 call-offs, 20 schedule-months, 610 daily census entries). OT spend is extremely high — spiked to $1.6M in March before easing slightly to $1.5M in April (-7% MoM). Clock variance worsening: 13.9% of nursing shifts >15min variance in April (up from 8.3% in Feb). Meal break missed rate 94.6% — very high, though only 463 shifts captured (T&A enabled subset).

integrationHealth.summary: Integration health tracking is not yet active. This dimension will score HRIS data pull recency, error rates, and uptime once a data source is connected. Note: an API integration discussion occurred Mar 20 — Aspen wants to pull scheduling/labor data into their UKG/GL analytics stack. API does not exist yet; Russell Dupaix will loop them in when building.

relationshipStrength.summary: Very active onboarding cadence: 16 meetings in 90 days, 5 in the last 30. Multiple team members engaged across facilities. Exec multi-threading is strong — President (Jay Brady, contacted today), CEO (Jeff Bradshaw), CAO (Marcus Paxman, contacted May 6), CTO, CFO, and two Executive Directors all in HubSpot. Q1 QBR scheduled May 14. Only 50% domain coverage (aspenhealth.com vs. gmail.com contacts). No in-person visits or past QBRs in window, but QBR imminent.

touchPoints.name: Aspen Kei-Ai South Bay & Veras | Intro & Welcome Call
Veras Check-In | Aspen Tracy Nursing and Rehabilitation Center
Aspen Lake Park | Veras Welcome & Setup Call
Veras Training | Aspen Tracy Nursing and Rehabilitation Center
Veras Welcome & Setup Call | Aspen Tracy (kickoff)
Veras Welcome & Setup Call | ASPEN SKILLED HEALTHCARE (Lodi)
Woodland Post Acute // Veras Welcome & Setup Call
Live building review — Ashley, Crystal, Maria

execEngagement.note: jobtitle coverage ~15% — some execs may be undetected. Strong C-suite presence: President, CEO, CAO, CTO, CFO all in system.

sentimentFriction.summary: NPS is alarming: avg 1.5/5 from Jan–Feb 2026 (Country Crest 1/5, Kei-Ai LA 2/5) — the two earliest live buildings. Circleback notes are largely positive (onboarding progress, engaged staff) but also surface a UKG sync gap, confusion around master vs. working schedule, and the API data gap. Two MEDIUM bugs in backlog — both very old (120 and 183 days). No HIGH open bugs. Intercom shows 0 open conversations (all 14 closed).

supportLoad.summary: 0 HIGH bugs. 2 MEDIUM bugs — both very old (183 days and 120 days in backlog). Also 10 open feature requests.

positive.text: Kei-Ai South Bay go-live on track for mid-May — platform fully configured, Myra confirmed login works
Marina Pointe team engaged — training progressing well with full team attendance
Lake Park excited about go-live target May 4 — all setups done
CAO Marcus Paxman actively engaged — requesting data integration for cross-facility analytics
President Jay Brady in HubSpot — contacted today (May 7)
Switched from OnShift — team preferred Veras for split shifts and PRN scheduling
Aspen published a month of schedules and some staff are using the app on live buildings
Q1 QBR scheduled May 14 — executive meeting confirms engagement at leadership level

negative.text: NPS 1/5 from Country Crest (Shoua Yang, Feb 2026) and 2/5 from Kei-Ai LA (Osvaldo Heredia, Feb 2026)
UKG sync gap: new CNA Cassidy Barnes did not sync from UKG into Veras (Ed, Feb 10)
Duplicate name publishing bug noted at The Avenues — manual workaround in place (Mar 17)
Marina Pointe team struggled with master vs. working schedule concept (Apr 17)
API integration Aspen requested does not exist yet — gap for their analytics reporting
Clock-in variance worsening: 13.9% of shifts >15min in April (up from 8.3% in Feb)

neutral.text: Aspen turned off time-off requests and shift swaps on early live buildings
Staffing budgets skipped on Lodi kickoff call — to be covered in follow-up
